metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: incapable (429 turn-end never persisted to messages.jsonl); driver_capability: gap:429-mock

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: gap:429-mock
route: frozen

## Reasoning

Mistral Vibe DOES hit token/quota exhaustion: when the Mistral API returns HTTP 429, `vibe/core/agent_loop.py` raises a `RateLimitError` (agent_loop.py:1692-1693 non-streaming and 1772-1773 streaming, gated by `_should_raise_rate_limit_error` at agent_loop.py:232-233 → `BackendError.status == 429`). The retryable-error set in `vibe/core/utils/retry.py:23` includes 429, but once retries are exhausted the `RateLimitError` surfaces. So `agent_supports = yes`.

**Why daemon = incapable (not full, not bug).** The irrlicht vibe adapter is a transcript tailer: `FilesUnderRoot` on `~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/messages.jsonl` plus the `meta.json` sidecar (core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/agent.go, parser.go, sidecar.go). It derives state purely from persisted transcript lines — `role:user` → `user_message` (opens the turn → working), `role:assistant` with no `tool_calls` → `turn_done` (settles → ready). The daemon can only observe the scenario's `working → ready` arc if a turn-end line is written to `messages.jsonl`.

On a 429 that line is **never written**:

1. In `_chat` / `_chat_streaming` the assistant message is appended to `self.messages` ONLY on a successful response (agent_loop.py:1682 and 1765). The 429 raises `RateLimitError` in the surrounding `except` BEFORE any append, so no assistant/turn_done message ever enters the in-memory list.
2. `_conversation_loop` persists via a `finally: await self._save_messages()` (agent_loop.py:1097). `save_interaction` writes only the incremental `self.messages` tail (session_logger.py:298-306) — i.e. just the `role:user` prompt that was appended at agent_loop.py:1034. No assistant line, no turn_done.
3. The error itself is TUI-only: the Textual app catches the exception (cli/textual_ui/app.py:1922-1934), resolves it to a `RateLimitError` string via `_resolve_turn_error_message`/`_rate_limit_message` (app.py:1953-1966), and mounts an ephemeral `ErrorMessage` widget (app.py:1934). That widget is UI state, never persisted to `messages.jsonl`. The vibe parser has NO RateLimit/429/quota handling at all (grep of the adapter is empty) — unlike codex's `rate_limits` / `rate_limit_reached_type` snapshot path — so even the quota SEMANTICS are invisible.

Consequence in the daemon: after the 429 the last persisted line is `role:user`, so `SessionMetrics.LastEventType == "user_message"`. `IsAgentDone()` (core/domain/session/metrics.go:388-427) returns true only for `turn_done` / `assistant` / `assistant_output` — not `user_message` — so it stays false. `ClassifyState` (core/application/services/state_classifier.go:22-117) has NO wall-clock / idle → ready path; the only routes to ready are rule 2 (`IsAgentDone` → turn_done) and rule 3 (ESC/tool-denial, which needs `LastEventType=="user"` + interrupt flags the vibe parser never sets). Neither fires. The live vibe process stays alive (the TUI returns to the prompt), so there is no `process_exited` fallback either. **The session sticks in `working`; the scenario's `working → ready` / final `ready` is unobservable.** No transcript trace of the turn-end exists → `incapable`, not `bug` (the daemon does not mis-handle a trace; there is no trace).

**Context vs quota.** `meta.json` carries `config.auto_compact_threshold = 200000` — the CONTEXT-exhaustion path (a `ContextTooLongError`, auto-compacted / `/rewind` + `/compact`) is distinct from this QUOTA (429) path. Both are surfaced only as TUI `ErrorMessage` widgets and neither is persisted, so the daemon can observe neither as a state transition. This cell is strictly the 429 quota-exhausted variant.

## Driver pillar (secondary — the cell is frozen on daemon regardless)

The interactive driver `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` is freshly scaffolded: `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` and `wait_turn` is stubbed (`not_implemented`), so it cannot even detect turn completion yet. Separately — as with the codex sibling — there is no Mistral/OpenAI-compatible 429 mock harness analogous to `tools/onboarding-factory/recording/mock-openai-429` / `mock-gemini-429`, and no way to burn the real Mistral account quota on demand. So `driver = gap:429-mock` (a `wait_turn` port would also be required). This is moot for routing: even a fully-ported driver + a working 429 mock could not make the turn-end observable, because vibe writes nothing to the transcript on a 429.

## Route

`agent=yes` + `daemon=incapable` → **frozen**. Not recordable: irrlicht fundamentally cannot observe a vibe quota-exhausted turn settling to ready. The cell becomes recordable only if Vibe starts persisting the failed turn (an error/turn_done line in `messages.jsonl`) AND the adapter learns to settle on it — an upstream + adapter change, not tooling.
caveats: The vibe adapter has NO RateLimit/429/quota parsing (grep of core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/ is empty) — unlike codex's rate_limits snapshot. Even the quota semantics are invisible; the scenario's lifecycle arc is the only thing at stake and it too is unobservable.

On a 429, only the role:user prompt is persisted (finally-save at agent_loop.py:1097); the assistant/turn_done line is never written because RateLimitError raises before the append at agent_loop.py:1682/1765. The error is a TUI-only ErrorMessage widget (app.py:1934).

Session sticks in working: LastEventType stays user_message → IsAgentDone false, and ClassifyState has no idle→ready path. The live vibe process stays alive so there is no process_exited fallback either.
Context-exhaustion (auto_compact_threshold=200000, ContextTooLongError) is a distinct code path from quota (429); both are TUI-only and equally unobservable. This cell is strictly the 429 variant.

Driver is freshly scaffolded (DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep', wait_turn stubbed) and there is no Mistral/OpenAI 429 mock — but that is secondary: the daemon cannot observe the arc even with a working driver.
Becomes recordable only if upstream Vibe persists the failed turn to messages.jsonl AND the adapter settles the session on it — an upstream + adapter change, not a tooling task.

sources.note: 429 → BackendError.status==429 → RateLimitError raised in _chat/_chat_streaming BEFORE self.messages.append(assistant); no turn-end message ever enters the message list.
_conversation_loop appends only the role:user message then persists via finally: _save_messages() — so on a 429 messages.jsonl gains just the user prompt, no assistant/turn_done.
Only the incremental self.messages tail (LLMMessage list) is written to messages.jsonl. The RateLimitError is not in that list.
RateLimitError is caught in the TUI and rendered as an ephemeral ErrorMessage widget (_rate_limit_message); never persisted.

State derives only from persisted lines: role:user→user_message (working), role:assistant no tool_calls→turn_done (ready). No 429/RateLimit handling. Sidecar reads cwd/active_model/context_tokens only (not session_cost).
IsAgentDone true only for turn_done/assistant; ClassifyState has no idle→ready path. With LastEventType=user_message and no process exit, the session sticks in working.
config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, config.auto_compact_threshold=200000, stats.context_tokens present; stats.session_cost present but NOT read by the adapter (cost_nonzero=false).
Freshly scaffolded driver: DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep', wait_turn not_implemented. Secondary to the daemon=incapable freeze.

recipe.notes: Not recordable (route: frozen; daemon=incapable). Mistral Vibe DOES hit quota exhaustion (429 → RateLimitError, agent_loop.py:1692/1772), but the failed turn is NEVER persisted to messages.jsonl: RateLimitError raises before the assistant append (agent_loop.py:1682/1765), _conversation_loop's finally-save writes only the role:user prompt (agent_loop.py:1097), and the error is a TUI-only ErrorMessage widget (app.py:1934). With no turn_done line, LastEventType stays user_message, IsAgentDone is false, and ClassifyState (no idle→ready path) holds the session in working — the scenario's working→ready is unobservable. The vibe adapter has no RateLimit/429 parsing at all (unlike codex). Secondary driver gaps: the scaffolded driver stubs wait_turn (DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep') and there is no Mistral/OpenAI 429 mock (analogous to mock-openai-429/mock-gemini-429). The cell becomes recordable only if upstream Vibe persists the failed turn AND the adapter settles on it — an upstream + adapter change, not tooling. See assessment.body.
